name: First Nations Finance
chequeRange: Secured & unsecured business loans; payables/invoice/equipment finance; terms up to 3 years (amount not published, SME-scale)
ownershipGate: none - this is the key point. Australia's first Indigenous-OWNED non-bank lender, but it lends to ALL Australian businesses (sole traders, SMEs, listed cos, govt). The borrower does NOT need to be First Nations owned.
eligibility: Any Australian business with an ABN seeking working capital, stock/payables finance or asset finance.
goodsFit: The single best-fit Indigenous-finance source for Goods right now because it has NO borrower-ownership gate: Goods can borrow while still supporting an Indigenous lender (20% of profits to the Pauline E. McLeod Foundation), which also helps funders' RAP/Indigenous Procurement narratives. Caveat: pricing is commercial, not concessional, and max term ~3yr, so it suits working-capital/inventory bridging, not patient growth capital.

name: Aboriginal Investment Group (AIG)
chequeRange: Project/JV-scale; not publicly fixed (privately funded from own property & business portfolio)
ownershipGate: Not a hard borrower gate - AIG is 100% Aboriginal-owned and chooses projects via partnership/JV. Fit is via co-investment/JV into a community-benefiting venture, not a gated loan application.
eligibility: Aboriginal-led or Aboriginal-benefiting enterprises in the 7 northern NT regions; AIG runs community stores, housing, and notably the Remote Laundries social enterprise.
goodsFit: Strong strategic fit: AIG already runs remote-community infrastructure social enterprises (Remote Laundries is directly analogous to Goods' washing-machine line). A JV or on-Country partnership for the NT production plant heading to community ownership is exactly their model. Best pursued as a partnership, which also strengthens the First Nations-ownership story that unlocks the rest of this category.

name: Aboriginal Investment NT (NTAIC - Northern Territory Aboriginal Investment Corporation)
chequeRange: Large; ~$680M capital pool, statutory powers to make grants, loans, guarantees, JVs and form companies
ownershipGate: Statutory purpose is to benefit Aboriginal people living in the NT (self-management, economic self-sufficiency). Not a fixed 50% rule, but capital must demonstrably flow to/benefit NT Aboriginal people, so a non-First-Nations-owned company would need a strong NT-Aboriginal-benefit / community-ownership structure.
eligibility: Projects benefiting Aboriginal people in the Northern Territory; can lend, guarantee and co-invest as well as grant.
goodsFit: Goods operates in remote NT communities and is migrating production to community ownership - a credible NT-Aboriginal-benefit case, and NTAIC can structure as a LOAN or GUARANTEE (rare in this category). But it is a new statutory body with slow, governance-heavy deployment; treat as a 6-18 month unlock tied to the ownership transition, not an Aug cheque.

name: Indigenous Land and Sea Corporation (ILSC) - 'Our Country Our Future' program
chequeRange: Varies by project; funded by ~$45M/yr from the ATSI Land and Sea Future Fund
ownershipGate: Not a strict 50%-ownership borrower gate, but assistance must deliver economic/social/cultural/environmental benefit to Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander people, usually via Indigenous-controlled land/enterprise partnerships.
eligibility: National (urban/regional/remote); partnerships, brokering and funding for Indigenous land- and enterprise-related benefit, incl. agriculture, tourism, conservation, manufacturing on Country.
goodsFit: Plausible via partnership: an On-Country recycled-plastic production facility on Indigenous-held land delivering jobs and community ownership aligns with ILSC's economic-benefit mandate. Realistically a grant/blended structure on ILSC's own timeline, not repayable concessional debt, and not fast enough for 31 Aug.

name: Gulf Regional Economic Aboriginal Trust (GREAT)
chequeRange: Up to $250,000 (typical loan under $100,000) at subsidised interest rates
ownershipGate: Borrower gate by geography + Aboriginality: supports Indigenous individuals/entrepreneurs in the Lower Gulf region of QLD. Goods is not Lower-Gulf-based nor Indigenous-owned, so direct eligibility is unlikely.
eligibility: Indigenous entrepreneurs/ventures in the Lower Gulf (QLD) region; patient debt finance + mentoring; ~$30M debt-free balance sheet.
goodsFit: Model is a near-perfect template (patient, subsidised remote-community debt) but it is region-locked to the Lower Gulf and aimed at Indigenous individuals, so Goods is most likely OUT as a direct borrower. Worth a conversation only if Goods runs a Lower-Gulf community-owned production node with local Aboriginal ownership.

name: Torres Strait Regional Authority (TSRA) - Business Funding Scheme / Concessional Business Loan
chequeRange: Concessional loans below commercial bank rates (amount not published; SME-scale)
ownershipGate: HARD GATE: business must be at least 50% owned by Torres Strait Islander and/or Aboriginal person(s) who RESIDE in the Torres Strait region (or Bamaga/Seisia). Goods fails both ownership and residency.
eligibility: 50%+ TSI/Aboriginal-owned, operator resident in Torres Strait region/NPA, commercially viable business.
goodsFit: Not a fit for Goods directly (ownership + region gate). Listed for completeness as a verified concessional Indigenous lender; only relevant if a Torres-Strait community partner became the majority-owning entity for a local production node.

name: Aboriginals Benefit Account (ABA) - Beneficial Grants Programme
chequeRange: Up to ~$30M/yr pool for one-off projects; individual grants vary (seed funding for enterprise/economic outcomes)
ownershipGate: Benefit gate: projects must benefit Aboriginal people living in the NT. Applicant needs an ABN (sole traders excluded). Not strictly 50%-owned but must be NT-Aboriginal-benefiting, typically Aboriginal-controlled.
eligibility: Projects delivering economic development / enterprise / community / culture / land outcomes for NT Aboriginal people; now administered by Aboriginal Investment NT.
goodsFit: Grant (not repayable) so lower priority per Goods' debt-preference, but the NT On-Country production + community-ownership story is a real NT-Aboriginal-benefit case. Competitive annual cycle; unlikely to produce a signed commitment by 31 Aug. Pair with the Aboriginal Investment NT relationship since they administer it.

name: Wunan Foundation
chequeRange: Not publicly fixed; Aboriginal economic-empowerment investments/partnerships in East Kimberley
ownershipGate: Aboriginal-controlled foundation focused on East Kimberley Aboriginal people; engages via partnership/MOU rather than open lending.
eligibility: Aboriginal economic empowerment initiatives in the East Kimberley (WA).
goodsFit: Potential WA (Kimberley) community-partnership / co-ownership channel for an On-Country production node, and a credible First Nations-ownership partner. Not a debt provider; treat as a connector / JV partner that strengthens eligibility for other Indigenous capital. Slow, relationship-led.

name: Impact Investing Australia + Malu Pty Ltd - SEDI First Nations Capability Building Grants
chequeRange: Up to $120,000 per social enterprise (capability-building services, not cash to operate)
ownershipGate: First Nations gate: for First Nations social enterprises (Indigenous owned or controlled organisations with direct social benefit). Goods is not Indigenous-controlled today, so likely ineligible until/unless ownership shifts.
eligibility: Trading Indigenous-owned/controlled social enterprises; grant must be spent on intermediary services (financial, impact measurement, legal, consulting).
goodsFit: Mostly OUT today on the Indigenous-control gate, and it is capability funding (consulting services) not catalytic capital. Flag for the future: once community ownership crosses the threshold, this becomes accessible. Program not yet open as of mid-2026.

name: Equity Trustees - Indigenous Wealth / Native Title benefit trusts (PBC trustee channel)
chequeRange: Varies entirely by individual native-title trust deed and corpus
ownershipGate: No single gate - each native title / PBC community benefit trust sets its own investment mandate; many can invest in community-benefiting enterprises in their determination area.
eligibility: Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander native-title groups/PBCs whose trust deeds permit economic-development investment.
goodsFit: A real but bespoke route: a native-title PBC in a community Goods serves could invest its benefit-trust funds into a local production node (often the cleanest path to genuine 50%+ community ownership). Requires identifying the specific PBC/trust in a target community; Equity Trustees (and Perpetual) are professional trustees who manage many of these. Not a single signable source - a strategy.

name: Indigenous Business & Employment Hubs (Yarpa NSW + Darwin/Alice Springs/Adelaide/Perth/Hobart network)
chequeRange: N/A - free brokerage to financial services (no direct capital)
ownershipGate: Services are for First Nations businesses/entrepreneurs; no capital so no funding gate, but Goods would engage as a partner/intro channel, not a client.
eligibility: First Nations businesses nationally; the Alice Springs and Darwin hubs cover Goods' NT footprint.
goodsFit: Use as a NETWORK/INTRO channel only - the Alice Springs/Darwin hubs broker connections to Indigenous lenders, community partners and JV opportunities that could anchor the community-ownership structure. Zero direct capital and won't produce a cheque, but cheap relationship infrastructure for unlocking this category.

name: Community Sector Banking (Bendigo & Adelaide Bank JV)
chequeRange: Not published; tailored NFP/for-purpose lending facilities
ownershipGate: none on First Nations ownership, but sector-eligibility skews to not-for-profit / charitable structures
eligibility: Not-for-profit and for-purpose organisations; Australia's only dedicated NFP banking service, B-Corp certified, JV of Bendigo & Adelaide Bank with 38 NFPs.
goodsFit: For-purpose lending aligned to values incl. a 'First Nations Recognition and Respect' lending theme. Caveat: oriented to NFP/charity borrowers, so A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd (for-profit) may not fit cleanly; the Butterfly Movement charity arm could be the better borrower entity.

name: Wyatt Trust - Catalytic Local Investment Fund (CLIF)
chequeRange: Not published; patient debt / risk capital, expected small-to-mid ticket
eligibility: Structure-agnostic (business, social enterprise or NFP) meeting at least one of Pre-distribution / Purpose / Prevention; initial focus on businesses operating in metropolitan Adelaide.
goodsFit: Genuinely concessional, patient, repayable capital with no ownership gate and a social-enterprise lens, exactly the QBE-preferred shape. Main weakness: stated geographic focus is metropolitan Adelaide, so fit depends on Goods having SA-based production/operations to point to.

name: Aboriginal Investment NT (Northern Territory Aboriginal Investment Corporation, NTAIC)
chequeRange: Large capacity ($500M investment corpus); makes grants, loans, guarantees and equity/JV investments
ownershipGate: other: statutory purpose is Aboriginal-led development and benefit to Aboriginal people in the NT; not a strict 50% ownership test but funding must serve NT Aboriginal self-management/economic self-sufficiency, so a non-Aboriginal-owned Pty Ltd would need a community-ownership/partnership structure
eligibility: Aboriginal-led development and enterprise in the Northern Territory; broad powers to lend, guarantee, form companies and enter JVs.
goodsFit: Strong strategic fit for the remote-NT community-ownership pathway: can provide loans AND guarantees, and its mandate matches Goods moving production to community ownership. Could fund or guarantee an NT community-owned production entity. Caveats: Aboriginal-benefit gate; large statutory body, slow (needs Strategic Investment Plan in force).

name: Social Ventures Australia (SVA) - Impact Investing / advisory
chequeRange: Varies; structures and syndicates debt and blended deals (its closed Social Impact Fund did $0.5M-$6M+ syndicated debt)
eligibility: Social enterprises, charities, housing and Indigenous economic-development projects seeking impact capital; SVA acts as intermediary/structurer and co-investor.
goodsFit: Not a current open fund (the Social Impact Fund / Diversified Impact Fund is closed to new investments), but SVA still structures and syndicates social-enterprise debt and runs the catalytic-deal market. Useful as a structurer/syndicator for the AU$425K catalytic raise and to reach other lenders. Slow for a 31 Aug sign.

name: Westpac Foundation - Social Scale-up Grant
chequeRange: AU$300,000 over 3 years
ownershipGate: none on First Nations ownership, but eligibility gate: must be a not-for-profit social enterprise creating jobs for people facing barriers
eligibility: Not-for-profit social enterprises whose primary purpose is creating jobs/employment pathways for disadvantaged Australians (incl. Indigenous Australians).
goodsFit: Grant (not the preferred repayable form) and gated to NFP social enterprises. A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd (for-profit) likely fails the NFP eligibility test unless applied via the Butterfly Movement charity arm. Annual competitive round, so timing for a 31 Aug commitment is poor.

name: Social Enterprise Development Initiative (SEDI) - capability grants (Impact Investing Australia / Social Enterprise Australia; First Nations stream with Malu)
chequeRange: Up to AU$120,000 per enterprise (capability-building grants)
ownershipGate: none; a dedicated First Nations social-enterprise capability stream is delivered by IIA in partnership with Malu
eligibility: Australian social enterprises buying capability-building services from approved specialist advisers; competitive rounds.
goodsFit: Capability/advisory grant only, NOT capital for beds or the QBE match, so it cannot supply the AU$425K. Could fund investment-readiness/structuring advice that supports the raise. Flag: grant, not debt.

name: NT Aboriginal Business Development Program (ABDP) - Northern Territory Government
chequeRange: Grant funding (program tiers; not a large capital facility)
eligibility: Aboriginal-owned businesses operating in the Northern Territory.
goodsFit: Directly serves remote NT, but gated on Aboriginal ownership which A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd does not meet; only accessible via an NT Aboriginal-owned community production entity. Grant, not repayable. Included for completeness / future community-owned structure.

name: Indigenous Land and Sea Corporation (ILSC)
chequeRange: ~AU$45M/yr program funding (Our Country Our Future); primarily grants and asset acquisition, not third-party enterprise lending
ownershipGate: other: Indigenous benefit gate (assistance for Indigenous people to acquire/manage land, salt water and fresh water Country)
eligibility: Indigenous people/organisations acquiring and managing land and sea Country for economic, environmental, social and cultural benefit.
goodsFit: Weak fit: ILSC focuses on land/sea acquisition and management and runs its own subsidiary enterprises rather than lending to third-party manufacturers. Possible only if a community land/enterprise project bundles Goods production. Included as a named, real, but low-fit Indigenous-benefit source.

name: Small Giants Family Office (Berry Liberman & Danny Almagor)
chequeRange: Undisclosed; deploys across debt, equity and recoverable structures (operates dedicated debt funds e.g. Regenerative Agriculture Debt Fund)
eligibility: Mission-aligned impact ventures, Oceania focus; thematic emphasis on regenerative agriculture, renewable energy, water; invites mission-aligned approaches rather than open rounds
goodsFit: A genuine catalytic AU family office that runs concessional debt vehicles and can structure non-equity, patient capital. Theme fit is partial (their core themes are regen ag / energy / water, not furniture), but circular-economy / recycled-plastic and remote community angle is a plausible pitch. Relationship-driven, no public application.

name: McKinnon Family Foundation
chequeRange: Undisclosed; mix of grants and impact-investment loans (loans expected to be repaid)
eligibility: Environment and social-justice focus; combines philanthropy with repayable impact investment
goodsFit: Explicitly uses both grants and repayable impact loans, so fits the no-equity / repayable preference. Recycled-plastic diversion (20kg HDPE per bed) and circular-economy framing aligns with their environmental focus. Small foundation, relationship-led, no open round.
contact: Via AEGN member profile / foundation enquiry

name: Atlassian Foundation (Impact Investing)
chequeRange: Undisclosed; runs an impact-investing portfolio alongside grants and product/people support
eligibility: Impact-driven organisations; foundation favours tech-enabled scaling of social impact
goodsFit: An active AU corporate foundation with an explicit impact-investing arm (not equity-only) able to make catalytic commitments. Fit is weaker because their portfolio skews tech-for-good and education rather than physical-product manufacturing; include as an outreach long-shot, not a core target.

name: The Christensen Fund (Program-Related Investments)
chequeRange: PRIs in the multi-$M range (e.g. US$2M PRI into Raven Indigenous Outcomes Fund); also makes grants
ownershipGate: other - strong preference for Indigenous-led organisations and Indigenous-led funds (not a strict 50% legal test, but Indigenous self-determination is core criteria)
eligibility: Indigenous Peoples' self-determination, rights, culture-based economy; uses PRIs/recoverable grants as well as grants
goodsFit: One of the few funders that actually deploys PRIs/recoverable grants into Indigenous-led ventures, and historically ran a Northern Australia program. BUT it consolidated out of the Northern Australia region from 2015 and prioritises Indigenous-LED bodies, so A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd (not First Nations led) is a poor structural fit unless fronted by an Aboriginal community-controlled partner.

name: Aboriginal Investment NT (ABA-funded)
chequeRange: Business grants up to $100K (start-up) / $150K (growth); Community Impact & Innovation Grants $300K-$1M; Quick Response up to $20K
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations - business grants require an Aboriginal-owned business; community grants require Aboriginal community-led projects in the NT
eligibility: Aboriginal businesses and Aboriginal community-led projects in the Northern Territory; capital drawn from the $680M Aboriginals Benefit Account
goodsFit: Highly relevant geographically (remote NT, On Country production) and well-capitalised, but GATED: Goods' trading entity is not Aboriginal-owned, so direct business grants are out. Realistic path is a Community Impact & Innovation Grant ($300K-$1M) with an Aboriginal community partner as lead applicant for a community-owned plant. Grant, not repayable.

name: Australian Ethical Foundation (Community Grants)
chequeRange: Community grants (historically tens of thousands per grant)
eligibility: NFPs, social enterprises and registered charities in Australia; environmental focus areas explicitly include waste minimisation and more widespread recycling
goodsFit: Thematic bullseye on the recycling/waste angle (recycled HDPE diversion) even though it is grant-only and small. No ownership gate. Useful for a discrete piece of the plant (e.g. shredder/granulator) rather than the $425K stack.
contact: Via Australian Ethical Foundation grants page

name: Westpac Foundation (Inclusive Employment / Social Scale-up Grants)
chequeRange: Inclusive Employment Grants $50K over 2 years (up to 40 orgs); larger Social Scale-up grants in some rounds
ownershipGate: none - but First Nations enterprises are an explicit priority cohort
eligibility: Social enterprises and community organisations creating jobs/training for people facing complex barriers to employment, including First Nations people; next round opens 2026
goodsFit: No ownership gate and First Nations is a named priority; strong if Goods frames the On Country plant as creating remote Indigenous employment. Grant-only (fails the repayable preference) and round timing/competitiveness makes a signed commitment by 31 Aug improbable.

name: Social Enterprise Development Initiative (SEDI) - incl. First Nations stream
chequeRange: Capability-building grants up to $120K; $3.3M earmarked for First Nations social enterprises
ownershipGate: none for the general stream; the First Nations stream targets Indigenous owned/controlled social enterprises (administered by IIA with Malu Pty Ltd)
eligibility: Australian social enterprises with a defined social benefit, including trading Indigenous owned/controlled organisations; delivered by Impact Investing Australia; new round opened 8 May 2026
goodsFit: Government-backed, sector-wide, no ownership gate on the general stream, and explicitly names First Nations social enterprises. Grant (not repayable) and capped at $120K, so a top-up not an anchor; the open 2026 round makes a near-term decision possible but not guaranteed by 31 Aug.

name: The Ian Potter Foundation
chequeRange: Project grants (varies widely); strategic collaborative initiatives $10M-$50M over 5-15 years
eligibility: Australian charities/innovators across multiple program areas; supports social entrepreneurs and innovation
goodsFit: Large, well-resourced national grantmaker that backs social entrepreneurs and innovation; no ownership gate. Grant-only and process-heavy (poor fit for repayable preference and the 31 Aug timeline), but a credible source for a discrete innovation or community-infrastructure grant.

name: Bendigo Bank Community Foundation (formerly Community Enterprise Foundation)
chequeRange: Grants typically $20K-$100K
eligibility: Australian community organisations and social enterprises; funded by Community Bank profit reinvestment
goodsFit: No ownership gate and a community-development remit that suits remote-community projects, but grant-only and modest cheque size. A supplementary source for a single plant component or community deployment, not the catalytic anchor.

name: Balnaves Foundation
chequeRange: AU$150,000–$250,000 per annum (prefers multi-year)
ownershipGate: other — requires DGR Item 1 status or an auspice with a DGR-1 org (no First Nations ownership requirement)
eligibility: Australian orgs in education, medicine or the arts focused on young people, disadvantage and Indigenous Australia; DGR-1 required.
goodsFit: Explicit Indigenous-disadvantage stream at a cheque size that suits the raise; fit moderate as lanes are education/medicine/arts not furniture, so frame on Indigenous youth wellbeing/employment. Needs a DGR-1 auspice.

name: Sidney Myer Fund & The Myer Foundation — Small Grants Program
chequeRange: Small grants (typically up to ~AU$50,000)
ownershipGate: other — DGR/charity status required; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Small discrete projects for communities facing exclusion, with explicit emphasis on Indigenous Australians and isolated rural/remote areas.
goodsFit: One of the clearest category fits: program names Indigenous Australians and remote communities directly; suits a defined bed-delivery project. Cheque modest vs the $425K raise but match-eligible.

name: Westpac Foundation — Inclusive Employment Grants
chequeRange: AU$50,000 over two years
ownershipGate: none (registered charity / social enterprise eligibility; ~35% recipients rural/regional/remote)
eligibility: Community orgs and social enterprises creating jobs/training for people facing employment barriers; next round opens 2026.
goodsFit: Strong thematic fit — Goods is a job-creating social enterprise producing On Country; Westpac funds employment social enterprises in rural/regional/remote areas. Likely needs charity/social-enterprise registration; small but credible and match-eligible.

name: The Ian Potter Foundation — Community Wellbeing
chequeRange: Varies (tens to low hundreds of thousands; multi-year possible)
ownershipGate: other — requires DGR Item 1 + Tax Concession Charity status; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Australian-based orgs with both DGR-1 and TCC status; Community Wellbeing supports disadvantaged communities including remote/Indigenous.
goodsFit: Community Wellbeing lane can cover remote Indigenous living-standards work, but hard DGR-1+TCC gate means Goods must apply via a charitable auspice; competitive national funder, real but not targeted.

name: Fay Fuller Foundation
chequeRange: Varies (place-based, often multi-year)
ownershipGate: other — South Australia focus; DGR/charity status typically required; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: SA mental health, wellbeing and systemic-change initiatives, explicitly including partnering with First Nations people.
goodsFit: SA-only limit narrows fit to Goods' SA work; explicitly partners with First Nations communities on wellbeing/systemic change, aligning with the bed/health-housing narrative.

name: The Wyatt Trust
chequeRange: Varies (direct and partner grants; SA-focused)
ownershipGate: other — South Australia residency/benefit focus; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Relief of poverty/disadvantage for South Australians; funds housing support, employment initiatives and Indigenous student support.
goodsFit: SA-restricted but funds housing/material-hardship relief, mapping to beds as essential household goods; fit limited to Goods' SA footprint, likely via a charitable channel.

name: James N. Kirby Foundation
chequeRange: Generally small-to-mid (typically up to ~AU$50,000)
ownershipGate: other — DGR/charity status generally required; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Broad Australian charitable grant-maker funding social welfare, environment, medical and community projects since 1967.
goodsFit: Generalist funder with a history of community/Indigenous grants; plausible for a discrete project grant but not remote-specific, so moderate fit and needs a charitable auspice.

name: Gandel Foundation
chequeRange: Varies (large family foundation; mid-to-large grants)
ownershipGate: other — DGR/charity status required; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Family foundation funding community resilience, Indigenous, education, health and social cohesion; invitation and application pathways.
goodsFit: Major family foundation with a standing Indigenous interest and capacity for meaningful cheques; plausible but not remote-specific and partly invitation-based, so cold access by 31 Aug is hard.

name: Aboriginal Benefits Foundation
chequeRange: AU$500–$5,000 per project
ownershipGate: other — DGR/charity or auspice typically required; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Public Ancillary Fund supporting health, education, art, youth, disability and poverty in Aboriginal communities nationally, with focus on Aboriginal art/artists.
goodsFit: National remote-Aboriginal reach is on-point, but the AU$500–$5,000 cap makes it immaterial to a $425K raise and the art focus is a stretch; real but low-value.

name: Bennelong Foundation
chequeRange: Varies (mid-size; not publicly fixed)
ownershipGate: other — DGR/charity status generally required; no First Nations ownership requirement
eligibility: Private Ancillary Fund supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities via education, training, employment and community health/wellbeing.
goodsFit: Mission overlaps directly with Goods (Indigenous employment + wellbeing), but PAFs commonly fund by relationship/invitation not open application, so access risk is high; strong thematic fit if a warm intro exists.
contact: Refer via philanthropic adviser / PafGUIDE listing

name: Aarnja Foundation Future Fund
chequeRange: Small community grants (open year-round)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations — restricted to Kimberley Aboriginal Traditional Owners and Kimberley Aboriginal Community Controlled groups/orgs
eligibility: Kimberley Aboriginal Traditional Owner and ACCO groups only; year-round applications.
goodsFit: Geographically perfect for Goods' Kimberley/WA remote work, but applicant must be a Kimberley Aboriginal-controlled group — A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd cannot apply directly; only viable via a community-partner co-applicant.

name: Noongar Charitable Trust
chequeRange: Small-to-mid community grants
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations — supports projects run by ACCOs benefiting the Noongar community
eligibility: Projects run by ACCOs delivering direct benefit to the Noongar community of south-west WA.
goodsFit: WA-only and Noongar-specific (south-west, not remote NT/Kimberley), and applicant must be an ACCO — poor fit unless delivering through a Noongar ACCO partner.

name: Ninti One Foundation
chequeRange: Unknown (small charitable donations/grants)
ownershipGate: unknown — verify; charitable status likely required
eligibility: Charitable arm of Ninti One focused on remote Australia research/development and Aboriginal community capability.
goodsFit: Explicit remote-Australia and Aboriginal-development orientation aligns with Goods' geography and On-Country model, but grant mechanics and cheque sizes are unclear and likely small; treat as a relationship/connector lead.

name: Coles Nurture Fund
chequeRange: Up to AU$500,000 (grant OR interest-free loan)
eligibility: Australian business trading 2+ years, turnover <AU$25M, <=50 FTE, GST-registered, in the food and grocery sector
goodsFit: Offers genuine interest-free (repayable) loans up to $500K with NO ownership gate, which matches Goods' debt preference. BUT eligibility is restricted to the food-and-grocery supply sector, so a furniture maker likely fails the sector test unless framed around a grocery/community-supply angle.

name: Westpac Foundation
chequeRange: AU$50,000 - AU$300,000 (one-off and multi-year)
eligibility: Australian social enterprises / NFPs creating jobs and opportunities for people facing barriers; charity/DGR status typically required
goodsFit: Explicitly funds social enterprises that create jobs for disadvantaged communities, which is Goods' On-Country employment model. Note: in 2026 the four Westpac-group foundations pivoted headline funding toward childhood numeracy/literacy, so the social-enterprise stream may be narrower this cycle.

name: CommBank Foundation Community Grants
chequeRange: AU$20,000 (approx 180 grants per year)
eligibility: Community organisations doing wellbeing-focused work in Australian communities; staff-nominated program
goodsFit: No ownership gate and broad community remit, but cheque size is small relative to a $425K raise and the program is staff-nomination driven rather than open strategic capital.

name: Rio Tinto Community Giving / Sponsorships & Donations
chequeRange: Typically AU$5,000 - AU$50,000+ per operation (varies by site)
eligibility: Grass-roots and community organisations in Rio Tinto operating regions (Pilbara WA, Gove/Nhulunbuy NT, Weipa QLD, Gladstone QLD)
goodsFit: Rio Tinto runs site-based community giving in exactly Goods' remote footprint (Gove NT, Weipa Cape York QLD, Pilbara WA). Strong geographic overlap with Indigenous communities Goods serves; apply per-operation rather than centrally.

name: Fortescue Community Grants / Innovation Grants
chequeRange: Community Grants pool ~AU$270K (FY26); Innovation Grants for sustainable solutions
eligibility: Projects benefiting Pilbara (WA) communities; focus areas include Indigenous capacity building, quality of life, sustainability and innovation
goodsFit: Innovation Grants explicitly fund sustainable solutions and Indigenous capacity building in the Pilbara, where Goods operates remote WA. Recycled-plastic manufacturing fits the sustainability/innovation theme. Geographically limited to Pilbara WA.

name: Newmont Community Investment (Tanami, NT)
chequeRange: Varies; global community fund precedent up to multi-million; site grants smaller
eligibility: Communities hosting Newmont operations; Tanami mine is in remote NT on Aboriginal land (Warlpiri country)
goodsFit: Newmont's Tanami operation sits in remote NT among Warlpiri communities, overlapping Goods' NT footprint. Community-investment dollars target host-community resilience and development, aligning with Goods' bed deployment and local employment.

name: Glencore Community Investment (incl. Minara WA, McArthur River NT)
chequeRange: Site-based community grants (NSW/QLD coal; Minara/Murrin WA; McArthur River NT)
eligibility: Community organisations near Glencore operations; supports Close the Gap health/education/employment for First Nations
goodsFit: Glencore's McArthur River (NT) and Minara (WA) operations are in remote regions with First Nations communities. Glencore explicitly supports Close-the-Gap health and employment pathways, which maps to Goods' health-driver (bed -> hygiene) narrative.

name: Santos Community Partnerships
chequeRange: Varies; capacity-building partnerships
eligibility: Local organisations in Santos operating regions (Cooper Basin SA, NT, QLD) delivering programs addressing local priorities
goodsFit: Santos operates across remote SA/NT/QLD (Cooper Basin) overlapping Goods' geography, and funds capacity-building social-value partnerships. Fit is moderate; energy-company giving tends to favour place-based partnerships near operations.

name: Wesfarmers Corporate Community Contributions (Indigenous programs)
chequeRange: Part of ~AU$8M+/yr across ~35 partners; multi-year partnerships
eligibility: Indigenous-led organisations or those with significant Indigenous programs; emphasis on remote Indigenous employment and Indigenous business development; WA-weighted
goodsFit: Wesfarmers directs corporate giving specifically to remote Indigenous employment and Indigenous business development, a direct match to Goods' On-Country production-to-ownership model. Tends to be board-approved long-term partnerships rather than open rounds, so access is relationship-led.

name: Pratt Foundation / Visy Cares
chequeRange: ~AU$10M/yr across many grants; individual grants vary widely
eligibility: Community organisations, with stated focus areas including Environment, Aboriginal Advancement, food security and education; some geographic weighting to Visy manufacturing regions
goodsFit: Strong thematic alignment: Visy/Pratt is a packaging-and-recycling empire and the foundation funds Environment and Aboriginal Advancement, plus has existing Indigenous partnerships near Alice Springs. Goods' recycled-plastic, Aboriginal-community model fits both pillars.

name: Suncorp Community Grants (via FRRR partnership)
chequeRange: Up to AU$25,000 per project
eligibility: NFPs/community groups in disaster-affected remote, rural and regional Australia; disaster recovery and resilience focus
goodsFit: Weak thematic fit: tightly scoped to natural-disaster recovery/resilience rather than social-enterprise growth capital. Cheque size small. Only relevant if a Goods deployment is framed around a disaster-affected community.

name: IAG Community Partnerships / IAG Foundation
chequeRange: Partnership-based (e.g. multi-year agreements ~AU$120K over 3 yrs)
eligibility: Community partners aligned to resilience and First Nations financial-inclusion themes; partnership rather than open grant
goodsFit: Weak direct fit: IAG's First Nations work centres on financial inclusion and insurance literacy, not furniture/manufacturing. Listed for completeness as an insurance-sector corporate funder with active First Nations partnerships.

name: Origin Energy Foundation
chequeRange: Varies; NFP partnership grants
eligibility: NFPs using education, training and development to help young people, including Indigenous students and children in rural/remote Australia
goodsFit: Weak fit: scope is education/training for young people, not enterprise or product funding. Only relevant if Goods packages an On-Country training/skills component.

name: Telstra Foundation Connected Communities Grants (via FRRR)
chequeRange: Small-to-mid community grants (run with FRRR)
eligibility: Remote, rural and regional community groups building resilience, sustainability and digital/social connection
goodsFit: Weak-to-moderate fit: remote/regional remit overlaps Goods' geography but theme centres on digital connection and liveability rather than manufacturing or beds. Possible if framed around community connection outcomes.

name: Australia Post Community Grants
chequeRange: Up to AU$10,000
eligibility: Incorporated NFPs running community-led local projects improving mental health and wellbeing; 2026 round closes ~6 July 2026
goodsFit: Weak fit: small cheque and mental-wellbeing scope. Could be tangentially argued via the wellbeing/sleep angle of beds, but not a serious capital source for a $425K raise.

name: Bunnings Community Grants Program
chequeRange: Up to AU$10,000
eligibility: NFP organisations delivering practical local-impact projects; program opens 1 July 2026
goodsFit: Weak as cash, but Bunnings (a Wesfarmers retailer) is a plausible in-kind/materials partner for a furniture-and-materials enterprise. Small cheque size; better as a supplier/relationship play than catalytic capital.

name: CEFC (Clean Energy Finance Corporation) – asset & equipment finance / NAB Green Finance for Vehicles and Equipment co-finance
chequeRange: Asset-finance discount line ~$200M pool via partner banks (0.5% rate discount); direct CEFC deals typically $1M+
eligibility: Australian businesses investing in eligible decarbonisation assets including recycling and lower-emissions manufacturing plant and equipment
goodsFit: Goods' On-Country plastic-recycling plant (shred/melt/press) is recycling + manufacturing equipment, exactly the asset class CEFC and the NAB co-finance program discount. No First Nations ownership gate, debt not equity.
contact: https://www.cefc.com.au/ (enquiries via site); partner program via NAB Green Finance for Vehicles and Equipment

name: National Reconstruction Fund Corporation (NRFC)
chequeRange: Debt, guarantees and equity across a $15B pool; deal sizes generally multi-million
eligibility: Australian projects in 7 priority areas; 'renewables and low emissions technologies' explicitly includes recycling and sustainable construction materials; must show ability to repay / commercial return; no grants
goodsFit: Recycled-plastic furniture manufacturing sits in NRFC's recycling / sustainable-construction-materials priority. Can take debt or a loan guarantee only (no equity), keeping inside Goods' no-equity rule. No ownership gate.
contact: https://www.nrf.gov.au/ (Economic Resilience Program live for manufacturing/logistics)

name: Indigenous Entrepreneurs Fund (IEF) – plant & equipment grants (NIAA)
chequeRange: Capital grants for plant/equipment (no fixed cap published; combined with microfinance)
eligibility: Indigenous-owned businesses, prioritising remote and regional, to access plant and equipment and reduce start-up risk
goodsFit: Designed to fund exactly the kind of plant/equipment Goods' On-Country plant needs, in remote settings. But ACT Pty Ltd is not currently 50%+ First Nations owned, so only viable post-ownership-change or via an Indigenous-owned lead entity.

name: Indigenous Land Enterprise Infrastructure Fund (ILEIF) – NIAA / IAS Remote Australia Strategies
chequeRange: Up to $300,000 (incl GST)
eligibility: Indigenous entities; infrastructure supporting primary industry / land management in remote and very remote Australia (sheds, solar, water, yards etc.)
goodsFit: Funds remote enterprise infrastructure (sheds, off-grid solar, water) that an On-Country production facility could use, but the scope is framed around primary industry/land management and it gates on Indigenous-entity status, so a stretch for a furniture plant.

name: NIAA Local Investments Funding (IAS)
ownershipGate: other: invitation-only; targets removing local barriers to employment in First Nations communities
eligibility: Applicants must be invited by NIAA; urgent small-scale one-off local community needs that remove barriers to local employment
goodsFit: Could underwrite a local-jobs barrier (tooling, set-up) tied to On-Country employment, but it is invite-only via the local NIAA office and small-scale, so not a reliable capital source for the $425K raise.

name: Remote Jobs and Economic Development (RJED) Program – NIAA
chequeRange: Wage/job-creation grants; $299M expansion for ~3,000 additional jobs
ownershipGate: other: employers creating jobs in remote First Nations communities (community-need led)
eligibility: Eligible employers creating ongoing jobs in remote communities; current round closed 7 April 2026, program expanding to 6,000 jobs by 2030
goodsFit: Goods as a remote employer could fund On-Country plant jobs through RJED, but it funds wages/jobs not capital equipment, and the current application round closed in April 2026.

name: Capacity Building for Native Title Corporations (PBC funding) – NIAA
chequeRange: Part of $114.8M over 4 years; per-grant amounts vary
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations (Prescribed Body Corporate / native-title holder only)
eligibility: Prescribed Bodies Corporate to build organisational capacity and pursue economic opportunities from their land; closes 30 June 2026
goodsFit: Only relevant if Goods partners with a PBC as the applicant to set up On-Country production on their land; ACT Pty Ltd itself is ineligible.

name: Social Enterprise Development Initiative (SEDI) – DSS / Impact Investing Australia
chequeRange: Up to $120,000 (capability-building services only, not cash capital)
ownershipGate: none (open to social enterprises; dedicated First Nations round)
eligibility: Social enterprise or trading Indigenous-owned/controlled org with defined social benefit addressing entrenched disadvantage; additional $2.6M round opened 8 May 2026 with a First Nations round opening
goodsFit: Goods clearly qualifies as a social enterprise, but SEDI funds purchased services (financial/impact/legal/business advice), not plant or working capital, so it supports the raise indirectly rather than filling the $425K gap.

name: Industry Growth Program (IGP) – Dept of Industry, Science and Resources
chequeRange: $50K–$250K (early commercialisation) / $100K–$5M (commercialisation & growth)
eligibility: Startups/SMEs with innovative commercialisation in NRF priority areas (incl. recycling/sustainable materials); requires advisory engagement; CURRENTLY PAUSED to new applications
goodsFit: Recycled-materials manufacturing is an NRF priority and the grant funds commercialisation/growth, but the program is paused to new applications, so it cannot deliver a signed commitment now.

name: Growing Regions Program – Dept of Infrastructure
ownershipGate: other: applicant must be a local government entity or incorporated not-for-profit (excludes for-profit Pty Ltd)
eligibility: Capital works projects enhancing liveability/social cohesion in regional, rural and remote Australia; further rounds funded in 2026-27 budget
goodsFit: Could fund a community production facility's capital works, but A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd is not an eligible applicant type; would need an incorporated NFP / community-org lead.

name: Commonwealth Outcomes Fund – DSS social impact investing
chequeRange: $100M over 10 years; outcomes payments to states/providers
ownershipGate: other: proposals submitted by states/territories; First Nations-led responses encouraged
eligibility: Payment-by-outcomes for entrenched disadvantage (families/children, unemployment, homelessness); states invited to submit May 2026–Feb 2027
goodsFit: Outcomes-linked and First-Nations-friendly, but it pays for measured social outcomes via state-submitted projects on a 2026-27 timeline, not capital for a manufacturer, so it misses both the instrument fit and the 31 Aug deadline.

name: Indigenous Procurement Policy (IPP) / Supply Nation directory
chequeRange: Revenue via Commonwealth contracts (remote contracts: mandatory set-aside; $80K–$200K SME first-offer)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations (to register as a certified/registered Supply Nation Indigenous business)
eligibility: Indigenous SMEs (50%+ Indigenous owned) can win mandatory-set-aside Commonwealth contracts, especially for remote-delivered goods/services
goodsFit: A revenue/demand channel rather than capital: if Goods restructures to 50%+ First Nations ownership, beds for remote housing/health could be sold into Commonwealth procurement under the remote mandatory set-aside. Gated on ownership.

name: NT Business Investment Concessional Loans (Invest NT / Local Jobs Fund)
ownershipGate: none (but requires a satisfactory Indigenous Engagement Strategy and a Territory Benefit Plan)
eligibility: Businesses with significant NT presence, high growth/job-creation potential, sound governance; sectors include manufacturing.
goodsFit: Best-fit state vehicle for Goods: repayable concessional debt, NO First Nations ownership gate, explicitly covers manufacturing and remote regional growth, and an Indigenous Engagement Strategy aligns with the On-Country plant model.

name: Queensland Circular Economy Investment Program
chequeRange: AU$250,000 - AU$750,000 (max 70% of project cost, 30% co-contribution)
eligibility: Businesses, social enterprises, charities, NFPs, partnerships transitioning to circular-economy models in QLD.
goodsFit: Directly funds circular-economy manufacturing using recovered materials; Goods' recycled-HDPE bed production is a textbook fit, social enterprises explicitly eligible, no ownership gate.

name: Queensland Recycling and Jobs Fund - Industry Development (RJFID)
chequeRange: Up to AU$10,000,000 (max 50% of eligible capex)
eligibility: Commercial-scale QLD operations delivering significant resource-recovery/recycling impact, esp. difficult-to-recover materials.
goodsFit: Funds capital for recycling/reprocessing infrastructure at commercial scale; fits a QLD On-Country shred/melt/press plant, no ownership gate. Capex-focused so needs a QLD facility footprint.

name: Queensland Resource Recovery Boost Fund
chequeRange: Part of a ~AU$45M / ~AU$10M recycling pipeline (per-project amounts vary by round)
eligibility: QLD projects boosting resource recovery and recycling capacity; businesses and social enterprises.
goodsFit: Supports building recycling/reprocessing capacity for hard-to-recover materials like plastics; aligns with Goods' plastic-diversion model. No ownership gate.

name: WA WasteSorted Infrastructure Grants (Waste Authority WA)
chequeRange: Project-scale infrastructure grants (varies by round)
eligibility: Investment in WA recycling/processing and remanufacturing infrastructure handling WA-sourced waste.
goodsFit: Explicitly funds equipment for remanufacturing recovered materials into new products; direct match for Goods' recycled-plastic bed components from WA-sourced plastic. No ownership gate. Requires WA facility.

name: Green Industries SA - Circular Business and Market Development Grants
chequeRange: Up to AU$100,000 per project
eligibility: SA businesses, NFPs, social enterprises and research bodies accelerating circular-economy practices.
goodsFit: Social enterprises explicitly eligible; funds circular design, reuse and recycled-content production, fitting Goods' recycled-plastic manufacturing in SA. No ownership gate.

name: Green Industries SA - Business Sustainability Program funding
chequeRange: Varies (co-funded business sustainability projects)
eligibility: SA businesses improving resource efficiency and circular/sustainable practices.
goodsFit: Secondary SA circular-economy vehicle; non-dilutive support for resource-efficient/recycled manufacturing. No ownership gate. Smaller and capability-focused.

name: Queensland Social Enterprise Grants (Dept of Trade, Employment and Training)
chequeRange: Growth grants AU$5,000-AU$25,000; capability grants up to AU$120,000
ownershipGate: none (must be a QLD social enterprise)
eligibility: Established QLD social enterprises building capability and capacity.
goodsFit: Goods clearly qualifies as a social enterprise; non-dilutive capability funding, no First Nations ownership gate, but capped and capability-focused rather than capital. Requires QLD nexus.

name: Transforming Queensland Manufacturing Grants Program (TQMGP)
chequeRange: Up to AU$1,500,000 (matched)
eligibility: Small-to-medium QLD manufacturers advancing operations and expanding to markets.
goodsFit: Large matched manufacturing grant fitting Goods' production scale-up, no ownership gate. CAVEAT: Round 1 closed April 2026, so depends on future rounds; QLD manufacturing footprint required.

name: NT Aboriginal Business Development Program (ABDP)
chequeRange: Up to AU$30,000 (ex GST), once per 3 years
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations (must be an NT Aboriginal business enterprise)
eligibility: NT Aboriginal-owned businesses in start-up/development addressing community needs.
goodsFit: Strong mission alignment but GATED: applicant must be an NT Aboriginal business; A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd does not qualify. Accessible only via a community-owned On-Country entity. Small cheque.

name: Aboriginal Investment NT - Business Grants (start-up / growth)
chequeRange: Up to AU$100,000 (start-up) / up to AU$150,000 (growth)
eligibility: NT Aboriginal-owned businesses in start-up or growth phase.
goodsFit: Good cheque size and NT-remote alignment but GATED to Aboriginal-owned businesses; accessible only through a First Nations community entity, not ACT Pty Ltd directly.

name: Aboriginal Investment NT - Collective Impact Initiatives
chequeRange: AU$300,000 - AU$1,000,000+ (Round 1 shared ~AU$30M across communities)
eligibility: Medium-to-large Aboriginal community-led social and economic development projects in the NT.
goodsFit: Large pool well-suited to a community-led On-Country production initiative, but GATED to Aboriginal community-led projects; requires a community partner as lead. Round 2 EOIs open 3 Aug-15 Dec 2026.

name: WA Local Capability Fund - Aboriginal Business Round (incl. Clean Energy Incentive)
chequeRange: AU$5,000 - AU$50,000 (50% of eligible costs)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations (registered Aboriginal business on Supply Nation / Aboriginal Business Directory WA)
eligibility: WA Aboriginal SMEs in trade-exposed industries building capacity to supply government/private sector.
goodsFit: Manufacturing is trade-exposed and fits, but GATED: must be a registered WA Aboriginal business; A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd does not qualify. Accessible only via a WA Aboriginal-owned entity.

name: WA Aboriginal Economic Development (AED) Program - DPIRD
chequeRange: Co-designed project funding (varies; partnership-based)
ownershipGate: other - Aboriginal-led / partnership projects with Aboriginal people and businesses
eligibility: Projects unlocking business and employment opportunities for Aboriginal people in WA, delivered in partnership.
goodsFit: Partnership-based regional development vehicle that could co-fund a WA On-Country enterprise with a community partner. Not a direct cheque to ACT Pty Ltd; functions as a connector/partnership pathway. Verify current round.

name: QLD First Nations Business Loan / Start-Up Finance Packages (DWATSIPM enterprise development)
chequeRange: Loan amounts vary (business loan, start-up finance, invoice finance)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations (Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander owned businesses)
eligibility: QLD Aboriginal/Torres Strait Islander owned businesses seeking growth, start-up or cashflow finance.
goodsFit: Rare REPAYABLE state vehicle (matches Goods' debt preference) but GATED to First Nations-owned QLD businesses; accessible only via a community-owned entity, not ACT Pty Ltd directly.

name: SA Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisation (ACCO) Grant Fund
chequeRange: Varies (Closing the Gap capacity/economic grants)
ownershipGate: other - must be an SA Aboriginal Community-Controlled Organisation
eligibility: SA ACCOs delivering services/economic development under Closing the Gap.
goodsFit: GATED to ACCOs; A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd does not qualify; only relevant via an SA Aboriginal community-controlled partner entity. Listed for completeness of SA pathways.

name: Business Growth Program NT
chequeRange: Matched grants (amount varies by round)
eligibility: NT businesses investing in growth, equipment and capability.
goodsFit: General NT growth-grant pathway with no ownership gate; could co-fund equipment for an NT On-Country plant. Smaller, capability/equipment focus. Verify current round status.

name: Mparntwe Alice Springs Community Foundation (MASCF)
chequeRange: AU$1,000-$10,000 (Give2Give program)
eligibility: Locally-led groups/initiatives strengthening the Alice Springs (Mparntwe) community; EOI rounds; 100% of funds raised go to community (admin covered by a donor).
goodsFit: The first and only community foundation in the NT, based exactly where Goods deploys (Central Australia). Strong local connector and credibility signal; could fund a discrete Alice Springs deployment or community-ownership-transition activity. Cheque size far too small for the $425K raise and it is a grant, not repayable.

name: Fremantle Foundation
chequeRange: ~AU$5,000-$380,000 (via named/sub-funds, e.g. Bibbulmun Fund granted $380K in the Fitzroy River/Kimberley region)
eligibility: WA-focused community foundation distributing grants on behalf of named/donor funds; has funded Indigenous-owned social enterprises and Kimberley/Fitzroy River charities.
goodsFit: Directly relevant to Goods' WA (Kimberley/Pilbara) deployments and has a track record funding Indigenous social enterprises (e.g. Mabu Maya cafe in Broome) and Fitzroy River region orgs via the Bibbulmun Fund. Grant only, not repayable; likely needs a DGR1 auspice. Useful as a WA place-based partner.

name: Queensland Gives (formerly Queensland Community Foundation)
eligibility: Charities with DGR Item 1 status, annual revenue under $5M, based in QLD; regional grant-making streams cover Far North Queensland incl. Cairns, Cape York Peninsula and the Gulf.
goodsFit: Regional grant streams explicitly cover Cape York and the Gulf, where Goods deploys in remote QLD. Hard barrier: requires DGR1 status and QLD basis, so Goods would need a DGR1/QLD auspice. Grant only, not repayable, modest cheque.

name: Eyre Peninsula Community Foundation
chequeRange: AU$1,000-$15,000 (typical community grants)
eligibility: Community foundation serving the Eyre Peninsula region of SA; small community grants to local not-for-profit projects.
goodsFit: Place-based SA community foundation; only relevant if Goods runs an Eyre Peninsula deployment. Small grants, grant-only, regional scope likely too narrow for current Goods footprint (more APY-lands/remote NT focused). Lower priority.

name: Stand Like Stone Foundation
chequeRange: ~AU$5,000 grants (≈$75K across ~15 projects per round) + scholarships
eligibility: Limestone Coast (Mount Gambier) SA community foundation; community grants and scholarships, some with an Indigenous focus.
goodsFit: Established SA community foundation with some Indigenous-focused giving, but its geography (Limestone Coast) does not overlap Goods' remote deployment regions. Weak geographic fit; grant-only and small. Listed for completeness.

name: Community Foundations Australia (peak body / connector)
chequeRange: n/a (directory + sub-fund matchmaking)
eligibility: National peak body for ~40+ Australian community foundations; 'find a foundation' directory connects to place-based funds and named sub-funds.
goodsFit: Not a funder itself, but the fastest route to identify additional place-based community foundations and donor sub-funds covering Goods' NT/WA/SA/QLD deployment regions, and to navigate DGR1/auspice requirements. Use as a connector to map every relevant local fund.

name: Community Enterprise Foundation (Bendigo Bank)
chequeRange: AU$2,000-$40,000 (varies by program/Community Bank)
eligibility: National philanthropic arm of Bendigo Bank; channels community grants (incl. via Community Bank branches) to not-for-profits across regional and remote Australia.
goodsFit: National reach including regional/remote areas where Goods works, and Bendigo's Community Bank network has remote-town branches. Grant-only, not repayable, requires charitable/NFP eligibility; cheque too small for the raise but viable for specific community-deployment costs.

name: Green Industries SA - Circular Infrastructure Grants (and Circular Business & Market Development Grants)
chequeRange: AU$25,000-$300,000 (up to $500,000 for projects of state significance)
eligibility: South Australian businesses/NFPs/councils that recover, handle and process recyclable materials; private business funded to max 50% of eligible costs (cash match required, no in-kind).
goodsFit: Direct fit to capitalise plant/equipment for the On-Country plastics processing (shred/melt/press) in SA. Funds capex for recycling infrastructure that turns HDPE waste into bed components.

name: Circular Economy NT program (CENT) / NT resource recovery grants - NT Government
chequeRange: AU$50,000+ (50:50 co-contribution)
eligibility: NT businesses, local government and not-for-profits delivering projects that improve recycling/resource recovery; must remain operational 5 years.
goodsFit: Strongest geographic fit - Goods' flagship On-Country plant is in the NT. Could fund plastics shredding/processing infrastructure to divert HDPE and feed bed manufacture.

name: WasteSorted Infrastructure Grants - Waste Authority WA
chequeRange: Varies by round (WasteSorted has distributed $16M+ since 2017; infrastructure grants typically tens to hundreds of thousands)
ownershipGate: other - eligibility skews to charitable recycling orgs, NFPs, local government, peak bodies and research/edu institutions; trading businesses not always eligible for infrastructure stream
eligibility: WA-based projects that recover/reuse priority materials including plastics; applicant type matters (charitable recyclers/NFPs favoured).
goodsFit: WA is a Goods operating state. Could fund plastics recovery/processing infrastructure for a WA On-Country plant - best accessed via an NFP/charity partner given eligibility skew.

name: Queensland Circular Economy Investment Program (CEIP)
chequeRange: AU$250,000-$750,000 (excl GST) per grant
eligibility: Supports Queensland's transition to waste minimisation/avoidance; open to organisations driving circular-economy projects (part of QLD's $487M Waste Reduction and Recycling Activation Fund).
goodsFit: QLD is a Goods operating state. Funds projects that avoid waste and use recycled materials - fits both the plastics-diversion and recycled-content manufacturing narrative for a QLD plant.

name: Queensland Resource Recovery Boost Fund (RRBF)
chequeRange: Stream 1 up to AU$2M; Stream 2 AU$2M+ per project
ownershipGate: other - applicant must be a Queensland council, group of councils, or representative entity (not a standalone private business)
eligibility: QLD councils and representative entities (with industry partners) improving sorting/processing/recycling/remanufacturing.
goodsFit: Indirect fit: Goods could be the 'industry partner' to a remote QLD council to process plastic waste into bed components. Council-led, so Goods cannot apply alone.

name: National Product Stewardship Investment Fund (NPSIF) - DCCEEW
eligibility: Industry-led initiatives that establish/expand product stewardship schemes (has funded national mattress stewardship, TreadLightly, etc.).
goodsFit: Thematic analog: Goods' bed take-back/end-of-life recycling could be framed as a furniture product-stewardship scheme. Currently between rounds (competitive round closed); future rounds depend on budget.

name: Heart Foundation - Catalyst program / First Nations CVD Grant
chequeRange: Varies (research/partnership; First Nations CVD Grant is a defined research grant)
eligibility: Ventures, programs and research that reduce cardiovascular disease incl. RHD in First Nations communities; Catalyst partners with health-hardware ventures (e.g. Remote Laundries).
goodsFit: Exact thematic match: the Stretch Bed is health hardware that interrupts the scabies->Strep A->ARF->RHD pathway the Heart Foundation is funding via Remote Laundries. Strong strategic partner/endorser and a route to RHD-Endgame health-hardware money; less likely as direct capital.

name: Aboriginal Investment Group - Remote Laundries Project (AIG)
chequeRange: n/a (delivery/partnership, channels government and philanthropic health-hardware funding)
ownershipGate: other - AIG is itself an Aboriginal-owned organisation; as a partner it could host/front Goods for ownership-gated money rather than impose a gate on Goods
eligibility: Aboriginal-owned operator delivering health-hardware (laundries) into remote NT communities; partners with Heart Foundation and government.
goodsFit: Closest peer/partner: same scabies-to-RHD prevention thesis, same remote-NT footprint, same Catalyst backing. Potential delivery/distribution partner and a bridge to government healthy-housing/health-hardware funding (incl. ownership-gated pools Goods cannot access alone).

name: Channel 7 Telethon Trust (Telethon7) - WA
chequeRange: Varies (equipment, services and programs; 147 beneficiaries funded in 2026)
ownershipGate: other - recipient must be a charity/DGR; WA-children focus (Goods would need a DGR partner)
eligibility: Charities/orgs delivering medical, health and welfare benefit to WA children, incl. Aboriginal children's programs (e.g. EON Aboriginal Foundation funded 2026).
goodsFit: Beds reduce child scabies/skin infections (75% of remote kids affected by age 1) in WA remote communities - a clean WA child-health-hardware story. Constrained by DGR requirement and Oct/Nov beneficiary cycle.

name: Perpetual IMPACT Philanthropy Application Program (IPAP)
chequeRange: Varies (Perpetual distributes ~$100M/yr across 1,000+ trusts; individual grants typically tens of thousands)
ownershipGate: other - applicant must hold charity tax concession + DGR status (Goods would need a DGR partner)
eligibility: Australian DGR not-for-profits; applications open annually Oct-Dec; some trusts target health, environment and Indigenous communities.
goodsFit: Could fund a health or environment project anywhere in Australia via a DGR partner (Butterfly Movement). Timing (Oct-Dec apply, payments by June) and DGR requirement make it slow and indirect.

name: Lush Charity Pot (Australia)
chequeRange: Up to AU$15,000
ownershipGate: other - grassroots/NFP-leaning; prioritises small groups working on systemic/catalytic change
eligibility: Small grassroots groups in animal protection, human rights or environment; regeneration/circular projects fit the Environment channel; rolling applications. Has funded First Nations climate work (Seed) in Australia.
goodsFit: Circular-plastics + First Nations angle fits the Environment channel; small cheque only - useful as a top-up/credibility grant, not core raise. Rolling intake means it could land before 31 Aug.

name: Patagonia Environmental Grants (Australia-accessible)
ownershipGate: other - grassroots environmental groups; favours risk-taking off-the-beaten-track work
eligibility: Grassroots organisations doing environmental/regeneration work; Patagonia funds within Australia; rolling/region-based intake.
goodsFit: Plastic-waste-diversion / circular-economy framing fits Patagonia's environmental remit. Small cheque - a top-up and a brand-aligned endorser rather than a core funder.

name: Clean Energy Finance Corporation - Australian Recycling Investment Fund
chequeRange: Large (e.g. $16.5M PET plant, $75M C&D plant; fund up to $100M over 10 yrs) - typically $5M+
eligibility: Debt/finance for circular-economy recycling and waste-reprocessing infrastructure with emissions-reduction benefit; commercial/concessional terms.
goodsFit: Thematically perfect (circular-economy recycling debt, no ownership gate) but minimum cheque is far larger than Goods' ~$425K need - relevant only at a future, much larger plant-scale raise. Included for completeness, not a near-term fit.

name: Impact Investing Australia
chequeRange: N/A - connector/peak body, no direct cheque
eligibility: Australian impact ventures/enterprises seeking impact capital; free 'Looking for funding or investors' pathway and market-building programs.
goodsFit: National peak that maps and introduces impact-capital providers, including ownership-gate-free concessional debt funds. Best single front door to find more matched/repayable capital ahead of 31 Aug.

name: Impact Investment Fund (IIF) - investor/enterprise platform
chequeRange: N/A - matchmaking platform; cheques come from connected investors (varies)
eligibility: Ventures, NFPs, B Corps and social enterprises publish a profile; consent-based introductions from 580+ investors, fund managers, foundations and family offices.
goodsFit: Directory + warm-intro engine to a large pool of aligned capital, including debt/concessional providers and family offices with no ownership gate. Low-effort way to surface additional matched capital.

name: Social Ventures Australia (SVA)
chequeRange: N/A as connector; SVA-managed funds vary (excl. SVA Diversified Impact Fund already known)
eligibility: Australian social enterprises, Indigenous businesses and social-benefit suppliers; advisory, impact-investing intermediation and social-procurement support.
goodsFit: Long-standing impact intermediary that structures and syndicates blended/debt deals and runs social-procurement pathways - can both introduce capital and help win contracts that de-risk repayment.

name: Australian Impact Investments (Aii)
chequeRange: N/A - asset consultant/advisory, no direct cheque
eligibility: Investors and enterprises; impact strategy, opportunity assessment and capital-raising advisory (founded 2014).
goodsFit: Market-leading impact asset consultant with direct relationships to impact debt/concessional funders; can broker introductions to ownership-gate-free lenders and validate the raise.

name: Impact Seed (Western Australia)
chequeRange: N/A - advisory/network connector
eligibility: WA-focused impact enterprises and funders; bridges impact funders and investible enterprises via education and matchmaking.
goodsFit: WA-based connector (Goods operates in remote WA) bringing funders together with investible projects - useful local pipeline to concessional/impact capital for the WA footprint.

name: Toniic
chequeRange: N/A - member network; member cheques vary (HNWIs, family offices, foundations)
eligibility: Global action community of ~500 HNW/family-office/foundation asset owners across 25+ countries; members source deals peer-to-peer. AU-active (APAC gathering Melbourne 2025).
goodsFit: Member deal-flow network reaching impact investors who do concessional debt across the capital spectrum with no First Nations ownership gate. A Goods member-sponsor could circulate the deal to aligned asset owners.

name: AVPN (Asian Venture Philanthropy Network) - Deal Share Platform
chequeRange: N/A - deal-share network; member cheques vary
eligibility: 600+ social investors across 35+ markets (incl. Australia); Deal Share Platform/Live circulates investable deals to members and supports investment readiness.
goodsFit: Regional deal-share network that can syndicate Goods' raise to foundations and family offices comfortable with concessional debt and no ownership gate. Good for aggregating multiple smaller commitments.

name: Social Impact Hub (Scaling Impact / investment-readiness accelerator)
chequeRange: N/A - accelerator/advisory; connects to investor pipeline
eligibility: Australian impact businesses with a clear SDG-aligned intention and intent to scale/become investible; advisory, accelerator and impact-investor matchmaking.
goodsFit: Runs an investment-readiness pipeline plugged into impact investors (incl. ownership-gate-free debt providers) and its Professional Impact Network - can sharpen the raise and broker introductions.

name: The Funding Network Australia (TFN)
chequeRange: ~AU$5K-$50K+ pooled per event (grant-style + 'sharks' offering debt/equity/philanthropy)
eligibility: Grassroots NFPs and social enterprises selected to pitch at live crowdfunding events; blended-finance format with debt/equity/philanthropy 'sharks'.
goodsFit: Live crowdfunding can rapidly aggregate many small commitments plus warm intros to debt providers in the room. Useful catalytic top-up, but selection + event timing make a signed commitment by 31 Aug improbable.

name: Social Traders
chequeRange: N/A - certifier/social-procurement connector (revenue not capital)
eligibility: Certified Australian social enterprises; connects 750+ certified SEs to 150+ corporate/government buyers ($843m social procurement catalysed).
goodsFit: Certification + buyer network drives contracted revenue that de-risks loan repayment and strengthens the credit story for lenders. Indirect but real support for a debt-led raise.

name: Philanthropy Australia (collective-giving network)
chequeRange: N/A - peak body; member cheques vary
eligibility: Peak body for philanthropy; convenes collective-giving circles and funder networks across Australia.
goodsFit: Gateway to giving circles and funders who can provide catalytic grants or PRIs (program-related/concessional) toward the matched QBE stack. Network/credibility play rather than a direct lender.

name: Impact North
chequeRange: N/A - Indigenous business-development/connector (Business Builder Program is free)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations - programs are for Aboriginal/Torres Strait Islander-led enterprises
eligibility: Aboriginal/TSI-led enterprises across northern Australia (whole NT, northern WA, northern QLD); Aboriginal-controlled organisation.
goodsFit: Directly covers Goods' remote NT/WA/QLD footprint and the move toward community ownership, but its support gates on First Nations leadership - relevant once/if Goods restructures to 50%+ First Nations ownership.

name: Supply Nation
chequeRange: N/A - supplier-diversity directory (revenue/procurement connector)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations - registration/certification requires 50%+ (registered) or 51%+ (certified) Indigenous ownership
eligibility: Aboriginal/Torres Strait Islander-owned businesses (5,800+ verified); connects to corporate/government buyers.
goodsFit: Powerful procurement-revenue network but hard-gated on Indigenous ownership - A Curious Tractor Pty Ltd does not currently qualify. Flag for post-restructure, not the current raise.

name: Indigenous Giving Circle (She Gives / Australian Communities Foundation sub-fund)
chequeRange: Unrestricted grants (amount not published; pooled member giving)
ownershipGate: other - funds only initiatives that are 100% Indigenous-governed, owned and operated
eligibility: First Nations-led initiatives that are 100% Indigenous-governed, owned and operated; reparations/untethered-funding model.
goodsFit: Grant only (not Goods' preferred repayable form) and hard-gated to 100% Indigenous control, which Goods fails today. Listed for completeness; not actionable for the current entity.

name: Black Coffee Australia
chequeRange: N/A - volunteer-run Indigenous business network (no capital)
ownershipGate: 50%+ First Nations - network of Indigenous small-business owners
eligibility: Aboriginal/Torres Strait Islander business owners; grassroots network across 36 regions (~2,330 participants) building procurement and Indigenous chambers of commerce.
goodsFit: Connection/procurement network for Indigenous-owned businesses; gated on Indigenous ownership. Relevant for the community-ownership pathway and partner introductions, not direct capital now.

name: Queensland Social Enterprise Council (QSEC)
chequeRange: N/A - state peak body (membership/network)
eligibility: Social enterprises and purpose-led businesses in Queensland; peak body, directory and advocacy.
goodsFit: State peak covering Goods' QLD footprint; network credibility, partner introductions and ecosystem links to QLD impact funders. Not a direct lender.

name: SECNA (Social Enterprise Council of NSW & ACT)
chequeRange: N/A - state peak body (membership/network)
eligibility: Social enterprises in NSW/ACT; certification/verification, advocacy and ecosystem connection.
goodsFit: Peak-body credibility and access to accelerators/investment-readiness listings and funder networks. Useful for pipeline and verification rather than a direct cheque.

name: Mannifera (collective of Australian funders)
chequeRange: Grants from a pooled fund (members commit min ~AU$65K/yr each; $5m+ granted to date)
eligibility: Civil-society organisations advocating for a fairer democracy and economy; collective of 26 funders.
goodsFit: Weak thematic fit - funds democracy/economic-reform advocacy, not enterprise capital, and gives grants not debt. Included for completeness; not a match for Goods' raise.
